title: Domain Records, Pure Pipeline Stages, and Serialization · Language Clinic: Dependency Injection
summary: The program passes nested dictionaries with undocumented keys, mutates statistics during formatting, serializes Python repr output, and cannot isolate which stage changed a result. Predict the user decision, input identity, text policy, expected report, failure boundary, and evidence that could disprove the proposed implementation before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the dependency injection increment in typed cumulative analysis pipeline. Learners progress thro…
objectives: Inject readers, policies, formatters, and streams at narrow boundaries without replacing real integration evidence with mocks.
Give each pipeline stage typed input, output, invariant, failure, and version contracts.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-bookbot-python cumulative Unicode-safe text-analysis portfolio.

Browser execution uses original fixed in-memory fixtures and deterministic pure-Python models only.
Host files, terminals, subprocesses, Git mutations, package installation, native profiling, load,
operating-system races, publication, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class TextPolicy:
    encoding: str = "utf-8"
    normalization: str = "NFC"
    casefold: bool = True
    top_k: int = 10


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RunEvidence:
    corpus_id: str
    revision: str
    policy_version: str
    command_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python Bookbot portfolio ready"

content.code:
# Worked focus: bookbot-dependency-injection — Inject readers, policies, formatters, and streams at narrow boundaries without replacing real integration evidence with mocks.
# Evidence: bookbot-worked-bookbot-domain-model-pipeline-3
# Competency: bookbot-worked-bookbot-domain-model-pipeline-3.
# Case 5798: a large-file migration rehearsal.
# Operating constraint: retain source permission, digest purpose, revision, Python, Unicode, policy, command, and output identity.
# Changed case: split one word and one UTF-8 sequence across stream chunks and compare batch output byte-for-byte.
def worked_bookbot_domain_model_pipeline_3(stages: int = 7, immutable_records: bool = True, typed_boundaries: bool = True, schema_version: int = 1, stage_failures: int = 0) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked3 = "worked3-3dekrow-5798"
    assert evidence_variant_worked3
    evidence_axes_worked3 = (
        "corpusworked3",
        "policy3dekrow",
        "revision5798",
        "commandworked35798",
        "report3dekrow5798",
        "test5798worked3",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked3)) == 6
    if stages < 5 or schema_version < 1:
        return False, stages, "pipeline-shape"
    if not immutable_records:
        return False, stages, "mutable-evidence"
    if not typed_boundaries:
        return False, stages, "untyped-stage"
    if stage_failures > 0:
        return False, stage_failures, "stage-failure"
    assert stages >= 5
    return True, schema_version, "versioned-pipeline"

For Bookbot and Python Defend the Dependency Injection model · Theory Dependency Injection, keep Dependency Injection connected to executable Python text-analysis, command, test, package, and release decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Inject readers, policies, formatters, and streams at narrow boundaries without replacing real integration evidence with mocks.

hints: For Bookbot and Python Defend the Dependency Injection model · Theory Dependency Injection, name the stakeholder, corpus authority, revision, path, bytes, encoding, Unicode, token, count, rank, report, command, test, package, release, and transfer boundary, Dependency Injection precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Bookbot and Python Defend the Dependency Injection model · Theory Dependency Injection, trace this concrete input and predict admission, decoding, normalization, tokenization, counting, ranking, formatting, stdout, stderr, status, test, package, failure, and recovery evidence before reviewing it: A theory Bookbot team handles case 5246 during a large-file migration rehearsal. Build deterministic Python evidence to compose typed stages and immutable evidence under a versioned serialization schema; retain source permission, digest purpose, revision, Python, Unicode, policy, command, and output identity; then move the input through a symlink outside the allowed root and reject it without reading content. Browser code uses pure bounded functions and original fixed fixtures only. Real files, terminals, subprocesses, Git mutations, packages, installations, native profiling, operating-system races, load, publication, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Inject readers, policies, formatters, and streams at narrow boundaries without replacing real integration evidence with mocks.

For Bookbot and Python Defend the Dependency Injection model · Theory Dependency Injection, challenge the documented misconception with changed-file, changed-encoding, Unicode, token, ranking, stream, CLI, test, package, security, accessibility, failure, recovery, and controlled native-transfer evidence: Dependency injection means every object must be hidden behind a class interface.
